timeline.label: Q1 2026 results: revenue $51.0M (+16% YoY), free cash flow $40.9M, quarterly dividend $0.12/sh, 2026 adjusted EBITDA outlook raised to $210–230M
FY2025 results: revenue $199.1M (+81%), adjusted EBITDA $177.2M, consolidated free cash flow $120M, ~450 new easements
Multiple 8-K filings on land acquisitions and digital-infrastructure development agreements (1918 Ranch, PowerBridge options)
Up-C reorganization and IPO completion at $17 per Class A share; LandBridge Holdings retains Class B control
Initial public offering and listing on NYSE as LB
Formation transactions with Five Point Infrastructure and WaterBridge ecosystem alignment
LandBridge Company LLC formed as Delaware Basin surface land manager spun from legacy Five Point holdings

industry.structure: Permian Delaware Basin surface land is concentrated among a few large acreage aggregators (LandBridge, TPL, private sponsors). LandBridge charges fee-based tolls on easements, surface use royalties, and resource rights rather than taking commodity price risk directly.
industry.share_shift: LandBridge has expanded through bolt-on acquisitions (Reeves, Lea, 1918 Ranch) while WaterBridge builds produced-water infrastructure on related acreage; TPL remains the largest pure-play Permian surface owner.
industry.trend: Industrial intensity on Delaware acreage is rising: produced water, power, fiber, pore space, and prospective digital-infrastructure uses compete for surface access; sponsor ecosystems (LandBridge / WaterBridge) shape contract flow.
